text.title: Microservices Circuit Breaker Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for adding a circuit breaker to a downstream call.

step.name: identify-call
step.action: Pick one downstream call. Confirm sync network call with a meaningful fallback (cached / default / degraded).
step.decision-gate: If no fallback exists, STOP — don't wrap; add async/queue instead.

step.name: size-thresholds
step.action: Set failureRateThreshold (50%), slowCallDurationThreshold (2× p99), slidingWindowSize (50 calls), minimumNumberOfCalls (10), waitDurationInOpenState (10s), permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState (3).

step.name: wire-decorator
step.action: Decorate the call: `Decorators.ofSupplier(() -> client.call()).withCircuitBreaker(cb).withRetry(retry).withFallback(... fallback).decorate()`. Composition: Retry(Breaker(Call)).
step.decision-gate: If Retry composition is outer (Breaker(Retry(Call))), STOP and swap.

step.name: register-metrics
step.action: Register Resilience4jMetricsRegistry; expose resilience4j.circuitbreaker.state, resilience4j.circuitbreaker.calls. Add alert on state=open for >60s and >3 transitions/5min.

step.name: chaos-test
step.action: Inject downstream 5xx for 30s; confirm breaker opens; confirm fallback returns valid response; confirm breaker closes after downstream recovers.
step.output: Chaos test report attached to PR.
